title: Pleroma carvalhoi
taxonomy: Pleroma carvalhoi is a species of flowering plant in the heath family, Ericaceae. It is native to Brazil and is found in the states of Paraná and Santa Catarina. Pleroma carvalhoi is a shrub that grows up to 3 meters in height and has white flowers with five petals. The plant is a member of the genus Pleroma, which is characterized by its small, white flowers and woody stems. Classification-wise, Pleroma carvalhoi is a member of the order Ericales, which includes other heath family plants.

This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its reported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Pleroma carvalhoi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects.
